discussion_posts.name: LeadingTheAbyss
discussion_posts.content:
# 😇 Hints That Actually Help > - **Approach Hints ➤** 1, 2, 3 > - **Implementation Hints ➤** 4, 5
Hint 1. Think about how DFS or finding depth could solve this question?

Hint 2. Find the maxDepth of the binary tree. Try solving 104. Maximum Depth of Binary Tree first if needed.

Hint 3. Try to use recursion until you reach maxDepth - 1 and return that node.

Hint 4. Maintain a currentLength counter during recursion and keep using DFS until you reach maxDepth - 1. When you reach that level, you can return the current node:
if (maxi - 1 == currentLength) return root;

Hint 5. After recursing on both sides, combine their results as follows:
if (left && right) return root;
return left ? left : right;
This means if both left and right return non-null values, the current node is the lowest common ancestor. Otherwise, return the non-null child.

discussion_posts.name: mrjbarnett
discussion_posts.content:
What is meant by deepest leaves?

The two deepest leaves?  

What if there are more than two leaves at the deepest level?

What if there is 1 leave at the deepest level and 2 leaves at the second deepest level?

discussion_posts.name: sumit_verma_77
discussion_posts.content: `Hint:` If the left and right subtree heights of a node are the same, that node is the Lowest Common Ancestor (LCA) of the deepest leaves.

discussion_posts.name: Preksha_1909
discussion_posts.content:
consider this test case :
Input
root =
[1,2,null,3,4,null,6,null,5]
Output
[2,3,4,null,6,null,5]

one more tip 

1.) To solve the problem, you can first find the height of the tree. 
2.) Then, identify the nodes at the deepest level and find their Lowest Common Ancestor (LCA). 
3.) This approach ensures you locate the LCA of the deepest leaves effectively.
[solution](https://leetcode.com/problems/lowest-common-ancestor-of-deepest-leaves/solutions/5639638/beginner-friendly-no-use-of-hashmap-postorder-traversal-clearly-explained)
